articleSummary: This article is a satirical analysis of how tech startups squander funding through predictable management failures. The core thesis argues that companies lose money by engaging in performative actions—such as mandating culture books, hiring a “savior” CEO, implementing 360 reviews, and misapplying business literature—instead of focusing on real product work. Specific technologies mentioned include Jira as a recurring “fix” and data-driven management as a hollow mantra. The tone is a cynical rant, framed as an observation of “unicorns in the wild” (startups) with recurring metaphors like “raising the bar” (euphemism for hire/fire) and “book club” as a signal of impending dysfunction. The intended audience is tech industry insiders (engineers, startup employees) familiar with these tropes.
